objectives: Administer users, permissions, processes, and services on Linux systems
Troubleshoot common system issues using core Linux tooling
Apply engineering principles to operational hygiene and repeatability
learning_path.title: Linux Foundations for Reliable Operations

objectives: Write Bash scripts to automate routine operational tasks
Use Python for structured automation and safe system interactions
Create reusable scripts that support incident response workflows
learning_path.title: Automation with Python and Bash

objectives: Model infrastructure changes using Terraform workflows
Manage state and apply safe change practices
Align IaC practices with engineering principles for maintainability
learning_path.title: Infrastructure as Code with Terraform

objectives: Understand core cloud building blocks and shared responsibility
Map infrastructure needs to cloud primitives
Operate cloud resources with reliability and cost-awareness
learning_path.title: Cloud Platform Fundamentals (AWS/GCP/Azure)

objectives: Manage system configuration consistently across environments
Reduce configuration drift and improve repeatability
Integrate configuration management into operational practices
learning_path.title: Configuration Management with Ansible/Chef/Puppet

objectives: Build, run, and troubleshoot containerized workloads
Understand container networking and storage basics
Apply engineering principles to container image hygiene
learning_path.title: Containers with Docker

objectives: Understand Kubernetes primitives and control plane behavior
Deploy and operate workloads with basic reliability practices
Troubleshoot common Kubernetes operational issues
learning_path.title: Kubernetes Core Concepts and Operations

objectives: Design CI/CD pipelines that support reliable releases
Implement checks that reduce operational risk
Connect delivery practices to incident reduction goals
learning_path.title: CI/CD Pipelines for Safe Delivery

objectives: Instrument and collect metrics for services and infrastructure
Build dashboards that reflect service health and user impact
Use SLI/SLO thinking to prioritize monitoring signals
learning_path.title: Monitoring and Observability with Prometheus and Grafana

objectives: Design structured logging approaches for troubleshooting
Use tracing to identify latency and dependency issues
Correlate logs, metrics, and traces during investigations
learning_path.title: Logging and Tracing with ELK and OpenTelemetry/Jaeger

objectives: Run effective incident response using clear operational procedures
Practice triage, mitigation, and escalation patterns
Perform post-incident learning aligned with engineering principles
learning_path.title: Incident Response and On-Call Operations

objectives: Define SLIs and SLOs that reflect user experience
Use error budgets to guide operational and delivery decisions
Connect reliability targets to monitoring and incident practices
learning_path.title: Reliability Engineering with SLIs/SLOs and Error Budgets

objectives: Understand failure modes in distributed systems
Reason about latency, partial failure, and dependency behavior
Apply operational strategies that reduce distributed-system risk
learning_path.title: Distributed Systems Fundamentals for Operators

objectives: Apply six sigma methods to reduce operational defects and rework
Measure and improve incident and change outcomes
Standardize processes to improve reliability and predictability
learning_path.title: Process Improvement with Six Sigma Methods in Operations

matched_occupation.description: Dependability engineers ensure the reliability, availability and maintainability (RAM) of a production process or a system. They focus on improving the capability to function without interruption and operate when needed, and on increasing the easiness of repairing, replacing or upgrading the product.
matched_occupation.label: dependability engineer
